PAM Rehabilitation Hospital of Centennial Hills
6166 North Durango Drive Las Vegas, NV 89149
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
Were Language Line services provided to patient?
N/A - Patient's preferred language is English.
Patient is a 75 year old who identifies as female who presented to Henderson Hospital on 3/26/26 for severe deformity following multiple
previous operative procedures on her left foot. She had a significant hallux varus deformity, arthritic changes at the hallux MTP joint, at
this more fixed metatarsal head with cystic changes, multiple lesser toe MTP coronal plane malalignment through the lesser
metatarsophalangeal joints into varus as well as fixed hammertoe deformities of the 2nd through 5th toes. Given the significance of her
deformity she was having difficulty with shoe wear. Pt underwent Left foot, tendo Achilles lengthening with Hemi resection technique,
hallux varus correction with modified McBride bunionectomy and repositioning, hallux metatarsophalangeal joint arthrodesis, bony
exostectomy of proximal phalanx and first metatarsal, 2nd through 5th hammer toec orrection, 2nd through 5th metatarsal resection
arthroplasty (03/26/26). Pt is NWB to LLE as per doctor's orders. Pt currently unable to stand on her own. Pt needs assistance showring but
Left foot pain/ hammer toe s/p surgery on ceftriaxone
2014/2016 B/L knee OA s/p replacement 2022 b/l OA s/p replacement Ruptured ovarian GB surgery 1964 tonsillecotmy 1960 umbilical hernia

Acetaminophen (Tylenol (pain)) 325 MG, Give 650 mg (2 tablet(s)) by mouth Every Six Hours PRN Not to exceed 4gm Acetaminophen/24hrs
Albuterol Sulfate (Albuterol Sulfate) 2.5 MG/3ML, Administer contents of 2.5 mg (3 mL) via nebulizer every four hours - RT PRN Indication:
Amitriptyline HCL (Elavil) 25 MG, Give 25 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth at Bedtime
Aspirin (Aspirin (Chew)) 81 MG, Give 81 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Twice A Day Indication: myocardial infarction prevention
cefTRIAXone SODIUM (cefTRIAXone) 1gram in Water for Injection, sterile 10mL, Infuse 1 gm over 4 Minutes intravenously Every Day, Rate: 150
mL/Hour Reconstitute vial with 10ml of sterile water for injection; slow IV push over 4 minutes Indication: POST SURGERY
Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3) (Vitamin D3 25 MCG) Give 1000 units (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily
HYDROcodone/acetaminophen 5/325MG (Norco) 5 MG-325MG, Give 1 tablet(s) by mouth Every Four Hours PRN *Look Alike/Sound Alike* Not to
Hyoscyamine Sulfate (Hyoscyamine Sulfate) 0.125MG, Dissolve 0.125 mg (1 tablet(s)) under the tongue Three Times A Day
Loratadine (Claritin) 10 MG, Give 10 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily PRN Indication: allergic rhinitis
Losartan Potassium (Cozaar) 25 MG, Give 25 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily
Mag Hydrox/Aluminum Hyd/Simeth - UD (Maalox Plus 1200-1200-120MG/30ML) Give 30 ML by mouth Every Four Hours PRN Indication: dyspepsia
Magnesium Hydroxide (Milk of Magnesia (30 ML)) 2400MG/30, Give 2400 mg (30 mL) by mouth Daily PRN If no bowel movement in the last 48 hours
Montelukast Sodium (Montelukast Sodium) 10 MG, Give 10 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth in the evening
Ondansetron (Zofran ODT) 4 MG, Give 4 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Every Eight Hours PRN Dissolve on tongue. Indication: nausea and vomiting
Oxybutynin Chloride (Oxybutynin Chloride) 5 MG, Give 5 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Three Times A Day *Look Alike/Sound Alike*
pantoprazole SODIUM (Protonix) Give 40 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Twice A Day
Pregabalin (Lyrica) 150 MG, Give 150 mg (1 capsule(s)) by mouth Twice A Day Time Critical Indication: diabetic peripheral neuropathy
Spironolactone (Spironolactone) 25 MG, Give 25 mg (1 tablet(s)) by mouth Daily
ursodioL (ursodioL) 250 MG, Give 500 mg (2 tablet(s)) by mouth Twice A Day

colitis, arthritis bilateral hip replacement bilateral knee replacement who identifies as female who presented to Henderson Hospital on 3/
26/26 for severe deformity following multiple previous operative procedures on her left foot. She had a significant hallux varus deformity,
arthritic changes at the hallux MTP joint, at this more fixed metatarsal head with cystic changes, multiple lesser toe MTP coronal plane
malalignment through the lesser metatarsophalangeal joints into varus as well as fixedhammertoe deformities of the 2nd through 5th toes.
Given the significance of her deformity she was having difficulty with shoe wear. Pt underwent Left foot, tendo Achilles lengthening with
Hemi resection technique, hallux varus correction with modified McBride bunionectomy and repositioning, hallux metatarsophalangeal joint
arthrodesis, bony exostectomy of proximal phalanx and first metatarsal, 2nd through 5th hammer toec orrection, 2nd through 5th metatarsal
resection arthroplasty (03/26/26). Pt is NWB to LLE as per doctor's orders. Pt currently unableto stand on her own. Pt needs assistance
showring but able to eat independently.
CLOF:close supervision with bed mobility. MAX assist with toileting
lives ina Two story house with spouse, main bedroom is located upstairs.
On admission all medications werecontinued from home/ SNF / hospital .Basic Chemistry were ordered .The patient was referred to PT/OT/RT/ST
The patient was referred to Physiatrist for pain management and in house Rehabilitation.
The patient is alert awake, oriented not in respiratory depress.

Primary Dx: Hallux Varus deformity s/p multiple extensive surgical interventions
Pt s/p L foot tend Achilles lengthening with hemi-resection technique, hallux varus correction with modified McBride bunionectomy and
repositioning, hallux metatarsophalangeal joint arthrodesis, bony exostectomy of proximal phalanx and first metatarsal, 2nd through 5th
hammertoe correction, 2nd through 5th metatarsal resection arthroplasty (03/26/26). Pt is NWB to LLE as per doctor's order.
75 year old female presented to Henderson Hospital on 3/26/3036 for severe deformity following multiple previous operative procedures on
her L foot. She had a significant hallux varus deformity, arthritic changes at the hallux MTP joint, at this more fixed metatarsal head
with cystic changes, multiple lesser toe MTP coronal plane malalignment through the lesser metatarsophalengeal joint into varus as well as
fixed hammertoe deformities of the 2nd-5th toes. Given the significance, she was having difficulty with shoe wear. She wished to move
forward with operative intervention on this foot.
PMHx: CKD, AKI, GERD, CAD, asthma, HTN, fibromyalgia, anemia barretts syndrome, liver disease, dyslipidemia, urinary incontinence,
pulmonary psychosis, sleep apnea, ulcerative colitis, arthritis, B hip replacement, B knee replacement
- NWB for LLE (8 weeks; pt reporting ortho cleared her for weight bearing through knee for knee scooter)
- Fall risk: 6 falls in February (one required staples to head)
- Bedroom and bathroom on second floor (walk in shower, built in seat)
- pt reporting she has a room downstairs for hospital bed
» Husband will hook up a shower head to kitchen sink, maneuver outside, and shower in backyard ("I'm a nudist and it's legal, so that's
what we do after the surgeries")
PT Plan of Care- STG & LTG:
STG (to be accomplished in ~7 days from eval)
1. Pt to demonstrate all bed mobility: min A
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
2. Pt to demonstrate all pt transfers : max A w/ LRAD
1. Pt to demonstrate all bed mobility: SPV
2. Pt to demonstrate all pt transfers: CGA w/ LRAD
3. Pt to demonstrate gait training 10'+ : partial A w/ LRAD
Roll left and right: The ability to roll from lying on back to left and right side, and return to lying on back on the bed
Sit to lying: The ability to move from sitting on side of bed to lying flat on the bed
Lying to sitting on side of bed: The ability to move from lying on the back to sitting on the side of the bed with no back
Lying to sitting on side of bed comments:
Sit to stand: The ability to come to a standing position from sitting in a chair, wheelchair, or on the side of the bed
Chair/bed-to-chair transfer: The ability to transfer to and from a bed to a chair (or wheelchair)
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
Toilet Transfer: The ability to get on and off a toilet or commode
Toileting Hygiene: The ability to maintain perineal hygiene, adjust clothes before and after voiding or having a bowel
movement. If managing an ostomy, include wiping the opening but not managing equipment
Car transfer: The ability to transfer in and out of a car or van on the passenger side. Does not include the ability to
open/close door or fasten seat belt
Walk 10 feet: Once standing, the ability to walk at least 10 feet in a room, corridor, or similar space
Walk 50 feet with two turns: Once standing, the ability to walk at least 50 feet and make two turns
Walk 50 feet with two turns comments:
Walk 150 feet: Once standing, the ability to walk at least 150 feet in a corridor or similar space
Walking 10 feet on uneven surfaces: The ability to walk 10 feet on uneven or sloping surfaces (indoor or outdoor), such as
Walking 10 feet on uneven surfaces comments:
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
1 step (curb): The ability to go up and down a curb or up and down one step

unsafe d/t NWB LLE and reduced strength
12 steps: The ability to go up and down 12 steps with or without a rail
88. Not attempted due to medical condition or safety concerns
unsafe d/t NWB LLE and reduced strength
Picking up object: The ability to bend/ stoop from a standing position to pick up a small object, such as a spoon, from the

Wheel 50 feet with two turns: Once seated in wheelchair/scooter, the ability to wheel at least 50 feet and make two turns

Wheel 150 feet: Once seated in wheelchair/scooter, the ability to wheel at least 150 feet in a corridor or similar space

oT Plan of Care- STG & LTG:
1. Patient will be S/U with oral hygiene tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
2. Patient will be MIN with bathing tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
3. Patient will be MIN with toileting tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
4. Patient will be MIN with UB dressing tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
5. Patient will be MIN with LB dressing tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
1. Patient will be IND with oral hygiene tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
2. Patient will be CGA with bathing tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
3. Patient will be CGA with toileting tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
4. Patient will be SPV with UB dressing tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
5. Patient will be CGA with LB dressing tasks, using adaptive strategies/equipment as needed. MET
Pt. reported taking pain medication prior to session
Pt. greeted semi supine in bed, c/o 8/10 LLE pain, agreeable to therapy.
Pt. declined ADL this session. Scores taken from previous session and PT report.
Pt. given HEP for UB and LB strengthening to support strength in non-affected extremities while LLE is NWB. Pt. educated on HEP through
verbal discussion, therapist demonstration, and pt. return demostration.
Pt. end of session semi supine in bed, all needs met, CLIR, mountaineer alarm activated.
Eating: The ability to use suitable utensils to bring food and/or liquid to the mouth and swallow food and/ or liquid once the
meal is placed before the patient
Oral Hygiene: The ability to use suitable items to clean teeth. Dentures (if applicable): The ability to insert and remove
dentures into and from the mouth, and manage denture soaking and rinsing with use of equipment
Toileting Hygiene: The ability to maintain perineal hygiene, adjust clothes before and after voiding or having a bowel
movement. If managing an ostomy, include wiping the opening but not managing equipment
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
Shower/Bathe Self: The ability to bathe self, including washing, rinsing, and drying self (excludes washing of back and
hair). Does not include transferring in/out of tub/shower
Upper Body Dressing: The ability to dress and undress above the waist; including fasteners, if applicable
Lower Body Dressing: The ability to dress and undress below the waist, including fasteners; does not include footwear
Putting On/Off Footwear: The ability to put on and take off socks and shoes or other footwear that is appropriate for safe
Should Brief Interview for Mental Status be Conducted? Attempt to Conduct Interview with Everyone:
If no because patient is rarely/never understood, skip to CAM.
Continue to Repetition of Three Words
Repetition of Three Words - Number of words repeated after first attempt
Ask patient: / am going to say three words for you to remember. Please repeat the words after / have said all three. The words are: sock, blue and bed.
Now tell me the three words. After the patient's first attempt, repeat the words using cues ("sock, something to wear; blue, a color; bed, a piece of
furniture"). You may repeat the words up to two more times.
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
Temporal Orientation - Able to report correct year
Ask patient: Please tell me what year it is right now.
Temporal Orientation - Able to report correct month
Ask patient: What month are we in right now?
Temporal Orientation - Able to report correct day of the week
Able to report correct day of the week
Ask patient: Let's go back to an earlier question. What were those three words that / asked you to repeat? If unable to remember a word, give cue
(something to wear; a color; a piece of furniture) for that word.
Brief Interview for Mental Status (BIMS) Score
A. Acute Onset Mental Status Change: Is there evidence of an acute change in mental status from the patient's baseline?
B. Inattention: Did the patient have difficulty focusing attention, for example being easily distractible or having difficulty
keeping track of what was being said?
C. Disorganized Thinking: Was the patient's thinking disorganized or incoherent (rambling or irrelevant conversation,
unclear or illogical flow of ideas, or unpredictable switching from subject to subject)?
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
D. Altered Level of Consciousness: Did the patient have altered level of consciousness as indicated by any of the following
vigilant - startled easily to any sound or touch lethargic - repeatedly dozed off when being asked questions, but responded to voice or touch stuporous -
very difficult to arouse and keep aroused for the interview comatose - could not be aroused
GOALS: Pt. MET all goals prior to d/c.
DME: Knee scooter, Manual w/c with leg rests, 3:1 commode, hospital bed (per pt request)
Supervising OT: Vanessa Almanzor, OTD R/L
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare

Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
75 yo F admitted to Henderson Hospital on 3/26/26 for severe deformity following multiple previous operative procedures on her left foot.
She had a significant hallux varus deformity, arthritic changes. Pt underwent Left foot, tendo Achilles lengthening with Hemi resection
technique, hallux varus correction with modified McBride bunionectomy and repositioning, hallux metatarsophalangeal joint arthrodesis, bony
exostectomy of proximal phalanx and first metatarsal, 2nd through 5th hammer toe correction, 2nd through 5th metatarsal resection
arthroplasty, Pt is NWB to LLE. Pt remained weak and given NWB status was transferred to PAM rehab for intensive PT/OT and medical
management. Wound care consulted for eval.
subjective: edema to LLE improving. continue elevation
HEENT: EOMI, neg scleral icterus bilat,
RESP: symmetrical chest rise, non labored
SKIN: post op dressing C/D/I. CMS intact pin sites clean and dry L toes 1-5.
Please see document storage for details and pictures on wounds
L foot deformity s/ p reconstructive surgery 3/26/26
bilateral hip OA s/ p replacement
Diagnosis: Encounter for other orthopedic aftercare
LLE: keep post op dressing in place
notify MD if soiled or dislodged
